title: Dynamic Hashing for Priority Queue Operations
description: A visual explanation of how dynamic hashing techniques (like extendible hashing) can be adapted to manage the buckets of a Priority Queue, allowing for efficient insertion and deletion of elements with varying priorities.
relationships: Hash function maps a priority key to a bucket pointer in the directory
Directory entries point to data buckets which store sorted priority elements
Insertion into a full bucket triggers a split, creating a new bucket and updating the directory
Deletion from an underflowing bucket may trigger a merge/collapse, updating the directory and freeing buckets
